Në ditët e fundit në vend ka pasur pretendime se projekti lidhet me Jared Kushner, dhëndrin e presidentit amerikan, Donald Trump.
Në lidhje me çështjen, kryeministri Rama ka deklaruar se projekti në Zvërnec po ndjek të gjitha procedurat ligjore dhe mjedisore, duke shtuar se deri më tani nuk ka pasur një projekt përfundimtar apo studim të plotë të ndikimit në mjedis, ndërkohë ai u ka bërë thirrje protestuesve për bashkëbisedim.
Gjithashtu në media është raportuar se Prokuroria e Posaçme kundër Korrupsionit dhe Krimit të Organizuar Tiranë (SPAK) ka filluar hetimet për çështjen e Zvërnecit, ndërkohë prokuroria ende nuk ka publikuar një njoftim zyrtar.

Facebook Summary:

The headlines call it a luxury resort. The map calls it something else.
A development linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump, the son-in-law and daughter of the United States president, is rising on the coast of Albania, and most coverage frames it as a tourism venture on a beautiful underdeveloped shoreline. That is not wrong. It is just incomplete, because of what the deal includes and where it sits. The project includes Sazan Island. And Sazan is not a beach.

Sazan is Albania largest island, and until recently a military exclusion zone, a fortified base of bunkers and tunnels closed to the public for most of a century. It sits at the entrance to the Bay of Vlora, on the Strait of Otranto, the passage only seventy-two kilometres wide that separates Albania from the heel of Italy and connects the Adriatic to the Ionian and the Mediterranean. Sazan marks the gate of the Adriatic. [1] A chokepoint is a narrow place through which a great deal must pass, and whoever holds the ground beside it can watch, control, or close the passage. Every warship and cargo vessel entering or leaving the Adriatic passes through Otranto, and a position on Sazan looks out over all of it. This is why the island was coveted across the centuries by Rome, Byzantium, Venice, the Ottomans, Italy, and Germany, and why the Soviet Union based its only Mediterranean submarine fleet just across the bay in the 1950s. [2]

The geography has not changed. Only the name on the deed has. Albania is now a NATO member, the old Soviet base nearby is used by NATO and Turkish vessels, and the United States has positioned special forces in the country. For the alliance, holding Otranto matters because it is the door through which Russian naval vessels would pass to enter the Adriatic. [2] So the question is not whether a resort would be pleasant. It is what is actually being acquired. When a former military island at the entrance to a strategic sea passes from a state into the private hands of the family of a foreign president, the word resort describes the surface and hides the substance. What changes hands is a position on one of the most sensitive points in the Mediterranean, held now not by a government answerable to a public but by a private venture tied to the most powerful political family in the Western world.

Around this small piece of geography, several interests converge. NATO needs the chokepoint held against Russia. The Adriatic is part of the energy corridor feeding southern Europe, including a real and documented Israeli ambition to pipe Leviathan gas toward Europe through the region, though the specific route remains a plan and this is not a claim that the project is built for it. And there is the plainest interest of all, the commercial one of the family of the sitting American president, acquiring a strategic landmark at a price and a legal status no ordinary investor would be offered. That status is what Albanians have noticed. The government of Edi Rama granted the firm special investor status and changed the protected-area law in 2024 to clear the path, beside a protected wetland. For more than a week, thousands have protested in Tirana with inflatable flamingos and signs saying the nation is not for sale, demanding the construction stop and the prime minister resign. [3] Rama calls it lawful and a path to EU membership. [4] The government calls it progress. The street calls it the price of the country.

This is a pattern. A small state that wants something from the West, membership, investment, protection, finds that the currency of the relationship is access, to its markets, its alignment, and here its geography. The patron family name opens doors no ordinary investor could, and the host government grants what it would deny anyone else. The cost is paid in sovereignty, over the objection of the citizens who will live with it. The rulers sign. The ruled protest. So when the next report calls it a luxury resort, hold the map beside the headline. The headline shows a beach. The map shows the gate of the Adriatic passing quietly out of public control and into the hands of the family that governs the alliance superpower. Whether a single hotel room is ever built, the position has changed hands.
When the gate of a sea changes hands, who has really been given the key?

How Did A Military Base Become A $1.6 Billion Resort Plan?
This comment made me stop.
Most people think Sazan Island is just a beautiful island off the coast of Albania.
It isn’t.
It was a strategic military base filled with bunkers, tunnels and Cold War infrastructure.
Now it’s at the centre of a luxury resort development linked to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump.
Thousands of Albanians have taken to the streets in protest.
Some see this as a simple tourism and investment project.
Others see something much bigger.
They see foreign billionaires, political connections, law changes, special investor status and increasing outside influence over a country’s land, coastline and future.
Many protesters say this is about sovereignty.
Who should decide what happens to Albania’s protected coastline?
The Albanian people or powerful international interests?
That’s the debate now unfolding across Albania.
#Albania #JaredKushner #WorldNews #Politics #News

What happened in Albania this week was a massive, hard-fought win for the people, proving that relentless public pressure and unwavering awareness are the only true forces capable of dismantling elite greed.

It is truly a remarkable coincidence that Jared Kushner just happened to randomly pick a highly strategic former submarine base for a "luxury eco-resort."

The development of luxury resorts on Albania’s Sazan Island and the Zvërnec Peninsula were painted by the elite as a “tourism and investment opportunity” - but are quite obviously planned to serve a strategic purpose for Israel and the U.S. - due to their military history and highly sensitive geographical position. Essentially, an outpost for building "Greater Israel". It is a classic imperial bait-and-switch.

To grasp the sheer scale of the rot, we must understand that plutocracy and necrocapitalism are deeply related, as a plutocratic government creates the exact political conditions that allow necrocapitalism to exist and profit. While plutocracy describes who holds the power (the ultra-wealthy), necrocapitalism describes a method of making money-specifically, a form of capitalism where profit is derived from death, destruction, subjugation, and the exploitation of vulnerable human lives. This grim reality is the beating heart of our current imperial world order.

The movement ignited on May 23, 2026, when residents and activists in Zvërnec mobilized against illegal fencing blocking public access to the Vjosa–Narta Protected Landscape. This spontaneous resistance rapidly expanded as demonstrators utilized social media and public rallies to confront state-backed projects and assert their right to communal spaces.

The sheer scale of the grift is breathtaking to behold. According to Reuters, the project on the Zvërnec peninsula was slated to cost $1.6 billion and build 10,000 rooms, directly threatening the Vjosa-Narta protected wetlands. They want to just rock up and bulldoze a literal paradise just to lay down some concrete for the global elite's next playground and a military base underneath it.

The imperial war machine looks at a fragile, breathing ecosystem and sees only a blank canvas for its own expansion. BirdLife International reports that the Vjosa-Narta Protected Landscape in Albania, targeted by U.S. developers, is a critical habitat for over 200 bird species and endangered marine life, including the Mediterranean monk seal and the area's iconic pink flamingos.

And I’m sure the 3,600 nuclear-hardened military bunkers on Sazan Island would make fabulous wine cellars for wealthy tourists, and certainly wouldnt' be secretly repurposed as a forward operating base for Western intelligence agencies.

We should definitely trust that the U.S. empire and its Israeli proxy-who have spent the last year turning Gaza into a graveyard-are suddenly deeply passionate about providing high-end Mediterranean ecotourism to the Albanian people. This sickening pitch is the kind of brazen lie that only a heavily insulated political class could peddle with a straight face to the working class masses.

The reality of their investments is infinitely more violent. The Council on Foreign Relations documented that since October 2023, the U.S. has supplied Israel with over $12.5 billion in military aid, directly funding the wholesale slaughter of Palestinian civilians and the destruction of Gaza. That is precisely what their blood money actually builds in the global south.

But people globally are finally waking up to the interconnected nature of this menace. The fierce resistance shown by Albanian protestors is inextricably linked to the anti-colonial struggle of the Palestinian people, as both are fighting against a savage Western imperialism that thrives on displacement, environmental destruction, and death. We are all bound together in this shared global struggle.

The local outrage is completely justified. Marxist.com noted that over 800,000 Albanians have been driven out of their country by the economic ravages of capitalism since the 1990s, yet thousands returned to the streets to protest the Kushner resort in what has been dubbed the "Flamingo Revolution". People are finally getting fed up with this systemic capitalist rot.

The people on the ground clearly understand the dark gravity of what is unfolding. The Albanian populace has awakened to the parasitic synergy between global oligarchs and the systematic erosion of their borders. They have forged a desperate, unified front to repel this encroaching shadow, standing like a rusted iron bulkhead slamming shut against a rising tide of black oil, a grim testament to a nation clawing back its autonomy from those who would see it liquidated.

This people-powered pushback has actually managed to throw a massive spanner in the works. Legally, public pressure forced the Albanian government to suspend construction pending an Environmental Impact Assessment, while the anti-corruption body (SPAK) probes the project's privatisation and legislative changes removing protected status. Politically, the outcome remains deadlocked; despite united citizen protests, Prime Minister Edi Rama remains defiant, declaring the investment will proceed as long as he is in office.

Despite the sheer arrogance of their leaders, this grassroots momentum is a beacon of hope. The "Flamingo Revolution" in Albania is an undeniable victory of the working people over necrocapitalism, proving that grassroots movements can successfully defeat the parasitic forces that seek to commodify and militarise their homeland. We must learn from their courage today.

Every delayed bulldozer is a testament to the sheer power of human solidarity. The victory over the developers at the Zvërnec Peninsula proves that when ordinary citizens unite against the rogue state machinations of Washington and Tel Aviv, the ruthless, blood-soaked engine of necrocapitalism can stopped in its tracks.

The fight is far from over, but the path forward has never been clearer or more fiercely urgent for us all. Francesca Albanese posted this week on X: “Necrocapitalism seems invincible - but it is not! All we need is unity, coordination, perseverance. Keep Protesting. Striking. Boycotting. Litigating. Not just once. All the times needed till the Apartheid ends and the system which fed it too.” We must forcefully dismantle the war machine and reclaim our shared humanity before there is nothing left. This is the ultimate test of our generation, and we cannot afford to fail here.

We're craving a way to break this engineered coma by chucking a figurative rock at the empire’s propaganda machine. 'THE TRUST FALL' documentary is the perfect antidote to manufactured obedience. It rips away polished media facades, forcing us to witness the moral void of the U.S.-Israeli death cult. It’s a crowbar to pry open closed minds before the West burns everything.

When we share the truth, we light a countless torches in the darkness of imperial deception.

Thousands have protested for a seventh consecutive day in Tirana against a multi-billion-dollar luxury resort project backed by Jared Kushner’s Affinity Partners. The development targets Sazan Island and the ecologically sensitive Vjosa-Narta protected wetlands. Demonstrators raise concerns over biodiversity impacts and demand greater transparency and Prime Minister Edi Rama’s resignation. Albanian authorities have opened anti-corruption investigations into the project approvals. The government maintains it will deliver major tourism and job benefits.

[ ... ]
"The immediate spark is a 4-billion dollar luxury resort project, backed by Jared Kushner’s Affinity Partners and linked to Ivanka Trump, planned for Sazan Island and the protected coastal wetlands of Zvërnec — an ecologically precious stretch that happens to be home to flamingos, sea turtles, and nesting seals. The Albanian parliament quietly removed the ban on construction in protected areas back in 2024, laying the legal groundwork for what critics now describe as a sweetheart deal with powerful foreign investors and politically connected domestic interests.
[ ... ]
What began as regional resistance in the villages of Zvërnec and Nartë has mushroomed into a national movement, with thousands carrying inflatable pink flamingos — symbol of the threatened wetlands — and banners reading “Albania Is Not For Sale.” Solidarity demonstrations have been scheduled from Vlora to Milan, London, New York, and Toronto. Albanian anarchists have gotten in on the act too, replacing the eagle on the Albanian flag with a flamingo — a gesture that manages to be simultaneously irreverent and pointed. The movement has been dubbed the Flamingo Revolution, and its decentralized leadership and Gen Z aesthetics have drawn comparisons to other youth-driven protest movements internationally.

Prime Minister Edi Rama has responded by calling it a “hybrid war.” That’s the tell. When a politician reaches for that phrase, it usually means the protests are working.
